_note: Real, verified external data used for context & impact slides. Every figure is cited. The flight dataset itself (300,153 rows) was scraped from EaseMyTrip in Feb-Mar 2022 across India's 6 metro routes.

acquisition.target: Air India (100%) + Air India Express (100%) + Air India SATS (50%)
acquisition.significance: The dataset was scraped Feb-Mar 2022 -- the FIRST weeks of Air India under Tata ownership. This analysis is literally the new owner's opening diagnostic.

market_share_2022.note: IndiGo dominates. Air India + Vistara combined (both Tata) is the clear #2 bloc -- the merger thesis.

vistara_merger.structure: Vistara (Tata + Singapore Airlines JV) merged INTO Air India; SIA holds 25.1% of enlarged Air India
vistara_merger.relevance: Both carriers are in our data and are the ONLY two selling business class -- the combined premium engine of the merged group.

market_growth.note: India carried its highest-ever 110M+ domestic passengers in 2022, +52% YoY -- a market rebounding hard post-COVID. Every pricing/fuel point compounds across a surging base.

fuel_cost.note: Jet fuel is the single largest controllable cost for Indian carriers (~40% of operating cost), and ATF prices spiked hard through 2022 on the Russia-Ukraine shock -- making the fuel-efficiency thesis urgent, not academic.
fuel_cost.source: IndianOil ATF price history; CAPA India / IATA cost structure

fleet_2022.air_india: Mixed: A320 family (ceo+neo) domestic; B777/B787 widebody international
fleet_2022.vistara: A320neo / A321neo narrowbody domestic; B787 widebody
fleet_2022.modelling_note: We model domestic metro routes on A320-family narrowbody economics -- correct for the routes in this dataset.
